name: Timeline stepper

variants.name: horizontal
variants.description: Steps arranged left to right. Default. Use for most wizard and onboarding flows.

variants.name: vertical
variants.description: Steps stacked top to bottom. Use when step content or descriptions are displayed alongside each step.

step_states.upcoming: Step has not yet been reached. Indicator shows step number.
step_states.active: The step the user is currently on. Indicator is visually highlighted.
step_states.completed: Step has been finished. Indicator shows a check icon.
step_states.error: Step contains an error requiring resolution. Indicator shows an error icon.

active_step.note: Zero-based index of the currently active step.

sublabel.note: Optional short description. Only visible in vertical orientation.

disabled.note: Step cannot be navigated to or clicked.

usage_rules.rule: Use for linear sequential flows only
usage_rules.detail: The timeline stepper is designed for ordered, sequential multi-step processes. Do not use for non-sequential navigation or filtering between unrelated views — use tabs instead.
A timeline stepper must have at least two steps. A single-step process does not require a stepper.
Avoid more than seven steps in horizontal orientation. If the process requires more steps, group related steps into sections or use vertical orientation.
Step sublabels (descriptions) are only displayed in the vertical variant. Do not define sublabels for horizontal steppers.

usage_rules.rule: One active step at a time
usage_rules.detail: Only one step may be in the active state at a time. active_step must always point to a single index.
When a step is in error state, display an inline notification within the step's content area explaining what needs to be resolved. The error state alone is not sufficient feedback.
All step labels and sublabels must use sentence case.

usage_rules.rule: Use compact size for dense layouts
usage_rules.detail: Use the compact variant inside side sheets or when space is constrained. Do not use base size in dense contexts.

keyboard: Tab to navigate between interactive steps
Enter or Space to activate a step
accessibility.aria_current: page — applied to the active step
accessibility.aria_disabled: Set to true on disabled steps
accessibility.note: Completed and upcoming steps that are clickable must meet minimum touch target size of 44px.

confidence_blockers: If ticket uses horizontal orientation with sublabels, flag as usage_rule violation.
If ticket shows more than seven steps in horizontal orientation, flag and suggest grouping or switching to vertical.
If ticket has a step in error state without a paired inline notification, flag.
If ticket uses a single step, flag — a stepper is not appropriate.

embedding_text: Timeline stepper component. Navigation progress indicator for sequential multi-step flows. Variants: horizontal left-to-right default, vertical stacked with sublabels. Step states: upcoming numbered, active highlighted current step, completed check icon, error critical icon. Sizes: base default, compact for dense layouts. One active step at a time. Min two steps. Max seven steps in horizontal. Sublabels vertical only. Error requires inline notification.
